_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to construct our own historical interpretations using appropriate concepts, terms, evidence, and conventions of historical writing.

_source.w: We are learning to construct our own historical interpretations using appropriate concepts, terms, evidence, and conventions of historical writing.
y: Constructing our own interpretations is the ultimate test of our historical understanding.
Using the tools and conventions of the discipline makes our work credible and professional.
This skill prepares us for advanced historical study and for informed civic participation.
eg: Writing an extended historical response that integrates evidence, concepts, and clear argumentation.
Using historical terms and concepts — continuity, change, causation, significance, perspectives — throughout our work.
Constructing a narrative that is both evidence-based and analytically coherent.
Presenting an interpretation using multimedia — combining text, images, maps, and timelines.
Acknowledging alternative interpretations and explaining why we favour our own.
Reflecting on the strengths and limitations of our interpretation and identifying areas for further research.
